evidence: The paper uses random forest feature importance to "select variables with the top 11 average feature importance in each multi-omics data" (Section 2.2) and interprets biological meaning from rankings: "Among the selected bacteria and metabolic pathways used for the 30-day mortality prediction, 6 pathways were related to purine nucleoside biosynthesis. Nucleotide biosynthesis has been reported to be critical for the growth of bacteria in human blood." No corroboration via permutation, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or conditional importance is provided, and the heterogeneous omics data (529 bacterial species, 472 pathways, 287 lipids, plus metabolites and clinical labs with different scales and distributions) amplifies impurity-importance bias risk.
